2604.25053.title: Analyzing LLM Reasoning to Uncover Mental Health Stigma
2604.25053.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) are increasingly being explored for mental health applications, recent studies reveal that they can exhibit stigma toward individuals with psychological conditions. Existing evaluations of this stigma primarily rely on multiple-choice questions (MCQs), which fail to capture the biases embedded within the models' underlying logic. In this paper, we analyze the intermediate reasoning steps of LLMs to uncover hidden stigmatizing language and the internal rationales driving it. We leverage clinical expertise to categorize common patterns of stigmatizing language directed at individuals with psychological conditions and use this framework to identify and tag problematic statements in LLM reasoning. Furthermore, we rate the severity of these statements, distinguishing between overt prejudice and more subtle, less immediately harmful biases. To broaden the reasoning domain and capture a wider array of patterns, we also extend an existing mental health stigma benchmark by incorporating additional psychological conditions. Our findings demonstrate that evaluating model reasoning not only exposes substantially more stigma than traditional MCQ-based methods but it helps to identify the flaws in the LLMs' logic and their understanding of mental health conditions.
2604.25053.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在心理健康應用方面越來越受到探索，但最近的研究顯示，它們可能對心理狀況的個體表現出污名化。現有的污名評估主要依賴選擇題（MCQs），這無法捕捉到模型潛在邏輯中嵌入的偏見。在本文中，我們分析LLMs的中間推理步驟，以揭示隱藏的污名化語言及其驅動的內部理由。我們利用臨床專業知識對針對心理狀況個體的污名化語言進行分類，並使用這一框架來識別和標記LLM推理中的問題陳述。此外，我們對這些陳述的嚴重性進行評估，區分明顯的偏見和更微妙、即時危害性較小的偏見。為了擴大推理範疇並捕捉更廣泛的模式，我們還通過納入額外的心理狀況來擴展現有的心理健康污名基準。我們的研究結果表明，評估模型推理不僅揭示了比傳統的基於MCQ的方法顯著更多的污名，還有助於識別LLMs邏輯中的缺陷及其對心理健康狀況的理解。
2604.25053.author: Sreehari Sankar et.al.
2604.25053.authors: Sreehari Sankar, Aliakbar Nafar, Mona Barman, Hannah K. Heitz, Ashwin Kumar, Pouria Tohidi, Dailun Li, Danish Hussain, Russell DuBois, Hamed Hasheminia, Farshad Majzoubi
